title: echinocereus cinerascens subsp. ehrenbergii
intro: Echinocereus cinerascens subsp. ehrenbergii, also known as Ehrenberg's hedgehog prickly pear, is a subspecies of cactus belonging to the Echinocereus genus, characterized by its cylindrical stems covered in fine, yellow or white spines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ehrenberg's hedgehog prickly pear include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. Traditional herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
